etymology.description: Depicts vertical threads passing through the top of a loom -> ancient form of 經 (jīng, 'to abide by, to comply with')
etymology.interpretation: The character 巠(jīng, 'a rivulet') originally depicted threads going through a loom, symbolizing a path or guide. Over time, its application expanded in meaning to include the idea of things running or flowing through, thus lending itself to the concept of a small stream or rivulet.
